channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Germans mourn attack on Christmas market with no answers about why
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned a violent attack and their shaken sense of security after a Saudi doctor intentionally drove a black BMW into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers, killing at least two people, including a small child, and injuring at least 60 others.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, SARA ABOUBAKR and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect Luigi Mangione's looks captivate TikTok users after perp walk
item.description: TikTok is swooning over Luigi Mangione, the accused killer of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, after his perp walk in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk helped kill a congressional spending bill. But much of what he spread was misinformation
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's billionaire ally Elon Musk played a key role this week in killing a bipartisan funding proposal that would have prevented a government shutdown, railing against the plan in a torrent of more than 100 X posts that included multiple false claims.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Government funding difficulties create gloom for federal workers before Christmas
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Johnny Zuagar says he’s tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighs how much to spend on Christmas presents. “I’ve got to keep a poker face,” Zuagar, a statistician at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canyon de Chelly in Arizona will become latest national park unit to ban commercial air tours
item.description: CANYON DE CHELLY NATIONAL MONUMENT, Ariz. (AP) — Commercial air tours will soon be prohibited over Canyon de Chelly National Monument in northeastern Arizona under a plan approved this week by the Federal Aviation Administration and the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Missouri governor commutes sentence of white police officer convicted of fatally shooting Black man
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A white former Kansas City police officer who was convicted of involuntary manslaughter in the fatal shooting of a Black man was released from prison Friday after Missouri’s governor commuted his sentence to parole. The decision by Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KTLA 5

item.title: Indiana man sentenced to the maximum of 130 years in prison for 2017 killings of 2 teenage girls
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2017 killings of two teenage girls who vanished during a winter hike was sentenced to a maximum of 130 years in prison Friday in the case that’s long cast a shadow over the teens’ small hometown of Delphi. Allen, 52, was convicted on Nov.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former school resource officer accused of sexually assaulting students
item.description: Stephen J. Carroll is accused of raping multiple students while working as a school resource officer in Scranton, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: US flu season is underway, as cases surge in some areas and vaccinations lag
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. flu season is underway, with cases surging across much of the country, health officials said Friday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention noted sharp increases in several measures, including lab tests and emergency room visits.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Starbucks workers plan strikes that could hit hundreds of stores by Christmas Eve
item.description: Workers at Starbucks stores plan to go on a five-day strike starting Friday to protest lack of progress in contract negotiations with the company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Michigan boss stabbed; copycat motive not ruled out
item.description: Court documents reveal new details about the stabbing of a company president by one of his own employees.
item.dc:creator: Byron Tollefson, NewsNation

item.title: Obama reveals his favorite books of 2024
item.description: With 2024 coming to a close, former President Obama is again sharing out a list of his favorites, posting his ten favorite books of the year.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Traveling for the holidays? These are the busiest times to fly or drive
item.description: Drivers and airline passengers without reindeer and sleighs better make a dash for it: It's beginning to look like another record for holiday travel in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Koenig, KRON4

item.title: Democrats say lines of communication open with GOP on funding, but no bill presented
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) told his party that lines of communication have reopened between his office and Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) to avert a government shutdown this weekend, members said.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Traveling? You can still watch NewsNation
item.description: The holiday season means millions of Americans will be traveling to see family across the country. But what if your relatives don’t have cable? How will you watch NewsNation? Thankfully, you’ve got options.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump adds Europe to the list of US trade partners he's threatening with tariffs
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday added the 27 countries that make up the European Union to the list of trade partners he's threatening with tariffs — unless the group takes steps to import more U.S. goods.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US civil rights probe faults Philadelphia school district over handling of antisemitism reports
item.description: The Philadelphia school district failed to show it protected Jewish students from harassment despite “repeated, extensive notice” that students, teachers and administrators were engaging in antisemitic behavior, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Leap of faith: A few young women in US buck the trends by joining the ranks of Catholic nuns
item.description: PALMYRA, Pa. (AP) — It was a radical idea, and for many years she had been resistant. But as Zoey Stapleton, 24, walked down a darkened hiking trail, steps behind her parents, she counted back from three and “took a leap of faith,” revealing to them that she wanted to become a nun.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: International students urged to return to US campuses before Trump inauguration
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A growing number of U.S. colleges and universities are advising international students to return to campus before President-elect Donald Trump is inaugurated, over concerns that he might impose travel bans like he did during his first administration.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Johnson says plan C reached to avert shutdown, vote expected
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Republicans have arrived at a plan C to avert a shutdown and the House will vote Friday morning on the legislation. "Yeah, yeah, we have a plan," Johnson said Friday morning as he entered the Capitol. "We're expecting votes this morning, so you all stay tuned.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: More student loans canceled in final round of forgiveness before Biden leaves office
item.description: With the latest round of relief, Biden has now canceled an unprecedented $180 billion in federal student loans through existing programs, covering 4.9 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing faces federal charge that's eligible for death penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO was whisked back to New York by plane and helicopter Thursday to face new federal charges of stalking and murder, which could bring the death penalty if he's convicted.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, LARRY NEUMEISTER and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA updates definition of 'healthy' on food labels for first time in 30 years
item.description: A new rule from the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) will update what it means for food to be labeled “healthy” for the first time in 30 years, a move that aligns with current nutrition science and is meant to help consumers better navigate food labels.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: What is 'coffee badging?' Workplace trend seen as protest to return-to-office push
item.description: As the post-pandemic return-to-office push gathers steam, again, workers are quietly mounting a pushback of their own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, ABC 4

item.title: Kansas restaurant worker caught chopping meat on ground, owner explains why
item.description: The video shows an employee cutting up or hammering meat on the sidewalk or drive-thru pavement area right outside Tryyaki.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon workers strike at facilities around the country as Teamsters seek contract
item.description: Amazon says it doesn’t expect an impact on its operations during what the union calls the largest strike against the company in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, ABC 4

item.title: Tiki, 20-year-old blind cat, saved in 'miracle' rescue from ice chunk in Massachusetts lake
item.description: A small black and white cat, who is blind, was rescued by good Samaritans after being stranded on a Massachusetts lake this week, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here are the most Googled Christmas cookie recipes in every state
item.description: Google Trends released a map of the most-searched-for cookies by state, and the recipe that's most popular in your state might just surprise you.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, ABC 4

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Grim new term coined to describe Oct. 7 phenomenon
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 500,000 winter tires recalled due to poor traction in snow
item.description: A tire company that sells winter tires has issued a recall for more than 540,000 of its snow tires, which do not comply with North American standards due to lack of proper traction on snow.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4
